action.reasoning: Observation: Previous training OOM during loss computation, even with batch_size=1. Analysis: The ForCausalLMLoss converts logits to float32, causing memory spike. Also visual tokens may be large. Decision: Rewrite train.py with memory optimizations: device_map='auto' with max_memory to use both GPUs, gradient checkpointing, smaller max_pixels to reduce visual tokens, manual loss computation in bfloat16 (avoiding float32 conversion). Train 2 epochs on full dataset, eval on val every 500 steps, merge best model, then submit eval. This should fit in 2×32GB and yield baseline grounding score.
